LAW AND MOTION, 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF RUSSELL BRIMER'S MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT PURSUANT TO CCP 664.6 IS DENIED, WITHOUT PREJUDICE. AMONG OTHER PROBLEMS, THE RESOLUTION OF THE DISPUTE IS ILLUSORY, APPEARS TO PROVIDE RELEASES FOR FUTURE BEHAVIOR, APPEARS TO PROVIDE RELEASE FOR OTHER THAN SIMPLY PROPOSITION 65 LIABILITY, AND DOES NOT NOW PROVIDE ANY BENEFIT TO THE PUBLIC. IT APPEARS THE PARTIES MAY HAVE RENEGOTIATED THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. JULY 18, 2012 RESPONSE OF DEFENDANT PROVO CRAFT TO ATTORNEY GENERAL, ETC., & EX. D APPENDED THERE. THE PARTIES MAY IN THE FUTURE FILE A NEW MOTION TO APPROVE. COURT SHALL PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: CURTIS E.A. KARNOW; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

FURTHER RESPONSES ARE REQUIRED AS TO THE SPECIFIC CUTTLEBUG BINDER THAT IS THE SUBJECT OF THE CERTIFICATE OF MERIT AND THE TOXIN LEAD, BUT NOT AS TO OTHER PRODUCTS OR TOXINS. INVESTIGATIVE ACTIVITIES UNDERTAKEN AT THE DIRECTION OF COUNSEL NEED NOT BE DISCLOSED. PARTIES TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, NOT REPORTED.

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-22-2012 Off Calendar

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-23-2012 Continued To Nov-06-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Approve Prop 65 Settlement Pursuant To Ccp Sec 664.6
Off Calendar Jul-24-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Clifford Chanler, Esq. and Josh Voorhees, Esq. of the Chanler Group, Tel. 510.848.8850, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party;
Thomas H. Clarke, Jr., Esq. of Ropers Majeski Kohn & Bentley, Tel. 415.543.4800, appearing for defendant Provo Craft via court call;
Harrison Pollak, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Tel. 510.622.2183, objector.

The matter was argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF RUSSELL BRIMER'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement Pursuant To CCP 664.6 is Denied, without prejudice. Among other problems, the resolution of the dispute is illusory, appears to provide releases for future behavior, appears to provide release for other than simply Proposition 65 liability, and does not now provide any benefit to the public. It appears the parties may have renegotiated the settlement agreement. July 18, 2012 Response of Defendant Provo Craft to Attorney General, Etc., & Ex. D appended there. The parties may in the future file a new motion to approve. Court shall prepare order. Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976

Off Calendar Oct-14-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Thomas H. Clarke, Jr., Esq., from Ropers, Majeski, Kohn & Bentley, 415-543-4800, for defendant and moving party
Stephen S. Sayad, Esq., from The Chanler Group, 415-388-1132, for plaintiff and opposing party

Argued. PLAINTIFF RUSSELL BRIMER'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents From Defendant Provo Craft & Novelty, Inc., and Request For Sanctions is granted in part and denied in part. Further responses are required as to the specific Cuttlebug binder that is the subject of the certificate of merit and the toxin lead, but not as to other products or toxins. Investigative activities undertaken at the direction of counsel need not be disclosed.
Parties to prepare order. Judge: Harold Kahn, not reported.

Disclosure made by Judge Kahn regarding Mr. Clarke, as a participant on another case pending before this court, with no bearing on the judge's ability to rule on this matter.

Argued. DEFENDANT PROVO CRAFT & NOVELTY, INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Re: Location Of Deposition Of Corporate Defendant is denied in part and granted in part. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Parties to prepare order. Judge: Harold Kahn, not reported.
